programme.title: Dusty at the BBC
programme.desc: A second chance to explore the BBC's archives with one of British music's biggest and best-loved stars, Dusty Springfield.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 24 September 1999 and featuring Vengaboys, Everything but the Girl, Paul Johnson, David Bowie and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme from Ikon, Newcastle, first broadcast on 1 October 1999 and featuring DJ Jean, Kelle Bryan, S Club 7 and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents a 1973 edition of the old-time music hall show from the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Bernard Cribbins, Sandie Shaw, Bill McCue and Frances Van Dyke.

programme.title: Wildlife on Two
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a profile of the pelican, the large water bird noted for its huge pouch and clumsy, waddling gait.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The victim of a road rage assault enlists the help of Hetty to find out the identity of her assailant.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Betrayed on his wedding day, Edmond Dantès is falsely accused of treason and condemned to years of brutal imprisonment in the Château d'If.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Escaping prison and uncovering a legendary fortune, Edmond reinvents himself as the mysterious Count of Monte Cristo and returns to a world that has prospered without him.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom. Hyacinth and Richard go to join the Major at a hotel for a golfing weekend. The Major cries off playing, but has organised a friend to play with Richard.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is suffering from bouts of sleepwalking, which could cause trouble as the annual interview for promotion is just around the corner.

programme.title: Albert Finney: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with archive interviews and classic clips. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents a 1973 edition of the old-time music hall show from the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Bernard Cribbins, Sandie Shaw, Bill McCue and Frances Van Dyke.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The victim of a road rage assault enlists the help of Hetty to find out the identity of her assailant.

programme.title: Wildlife on Two
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a profile of the pelican, the large water bird noted for its huge pouch and clumsy, waddling gait.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Sun
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Helen Czerski reveal the inner workings of the sun and investigate why scientists think changes in the sun's behaviour may have powerful effects on our climate.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: With Nikolaus Pevsner's Buildings of England in hand, Michael Bracewell explores the architecture of Surrey. Amongst the places he visits are Polesden Lacey and Whitely Village.

programme.title: Inside Classical: Dvorak's New World Symphony
programme.desc: The BBC Philharmonic performs Bruch's First Violin Concerto and Dvorak's New World.

programme.title: Attenborough's Passion Projects
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks back on his personal highlights. First transmitted in 1989, Lost Worlds, Vanished Lives explores the world's major fossil sites.

programme.title: Darcey Bussell on The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: Dame Darcey Bussell introduces us to a gem from the BBC's dance archives, The Magic of Dance, presented by celebrated ballet dancer Margot Fonteyn.

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn discusses the role of the male in dance. Her story is illustrated by some of the world's greatest dancers performing some of the era's most enduring dances.

programme.title: Sammy Davis Jr at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of some of Sammy Davis Jr's finest moments on British television - showing off his unbeatable talents as singer and raconteur.

programme.title: Confucius: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes visits China on the trail of Confucius, a great sage of Chinese history.

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: With Nikolaus Pevsner's Buildings of England in hand, Michael Bracewell explores the architecture of Surrey. Amongst the places he visits are Polesden Lacey and Whitely Village.

programme.title: Inside Classical: Dvorak's New World Symphony
programme.desc: The BBC Philharmonic performs Bruch's First Violin Concerto and Dvorak's New World.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The University of Bristol plays Durham University in the first match of the series, with a place in the second round at stake. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which patience and lateral thinking are as vital as knowledge, and connections need to be made between apparently unconnected things.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: Documentary series which unearths lost civilisations and cities. This edition visits northern Peru to find the ruins of 250 mysterious pyramids.

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce uncovers the story of Leonardo da Vinci. She travels to Florence, Milan, Paris, Warsaw and to New York, where she is given an exclusive preview of a new Leonardo.

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon tells the story of artist Walter Sickert, who understood that the theatre of war was not confined to the trenches.

programme.title: Lisbon: Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke reveal Lisbon's fascinating history through its cobbled streets, steep hills, picturesque shoreline and cultural riches.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: In the 18th century, trade and conquest funds an explosion of creativity, and art stirs a growing social conscience.

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce uncovers the story of Leonardo da Vinci. She travels to Florence, Milan, Paris, Warsaw and to New York, where she is given an exclusive preview of a new Leonardo.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: In the 18th century, trade and conquest funds an explosion of creativity, and art stirs a growing social conscience.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The Open University and Newcastle University face off for a place in round two.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which patience and lateral thinking are as vital as knowledge, and connections need to be made between apparently unconnected things.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about an irrepressible snob. Hyacinth's quest for perfection is somewhat hampered by the dubious antics of the rest of her family.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Sitcom. Timothy's handsome cousin Brinsley comes to stay - and supplants Timothy in Mother's affections.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Featuring the revolutionary period in Britain after the execution of Charles I, from Cromwell's iron rule to Charles II's attempts to restore the lustre of the monarchy.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Silvio Berlusconi's ambitious takeover of AC Milan reshapes the club and elevates his own profile, charting a path from footballing triumphs to the brink of political power.

programme.title: Saving Venice
programme.desc: How Venetians are turning to revolutionary engineering, restoration of the natural environment and knowledge from their past in order to save Venice.
